task objective: Make post-game Syncthing status monitoring passive, preserve it through gameplay, and distinguish absent configuration from configured API or folder failures.
tests added: Backend configuration classification for missing configuration, unavailable API access, and unmatched backup folders.
Configuration-aware detection grace calculations clamped to 30 through 120 seconds.
Frontend handoff tests for indexing without upload evidence, backend-provided detection grace, warning states, and exit behavior without process pause or resume.
A regression proving passive post-game watches remain alive beyond 120 seconds of gameplay and activate only after backup.
design decisions: Start the post-game watcher before backup without requesting a Syncthing scan, but buffer all publication until backup succeeds.
Treat peer upload evidence as the only post-game transition to SYNCTHING UPLOADING; indexing and local update states remain SYNCTHING PREPARING.
Use Syncthing filesystem watcher or rescan configuration to derive a 30 through 120 second upload-detection window.
Keep active Syncthing status states visible until the monitor explicitly replaces them.
Silently use normal local-backup success when Syncthing is not configured, while showing brief warnings for configured API failures or an unshared backup path.
Apply the 120-second active watcher watchdog only after post-backup handoff, so normal gameplay duration cannot expire the passive watcher.
results: 487 Python tests and 51 frontend tests pass.
Ruff, formatting, ty, TypeScript type checking, frontend supply-chain verification, Rollup build, package creation, and Deck deployment pass.
